For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework, within the listed date range:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open until September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects minimal implied probabilities for major bank failures by June 30, driven by resilient sector fundamentals despite pockets of commercial real estate (CRE) stress. Year-to-date, only two small institutions failed—Metropolitan Capital Bank & Trust ($261 million assets) on January 30 and Community Bank & Trust-West Georgia on May 1—citing localized CRE loan losses and liquidity issues, with FDIC losses under $20 million each. The FDIC's Q4 2025 Quarterly Banking Profile shows 60 problem banks (1.4% of total), within non-crisis norms, alongside robust net income growth and deposit inflows. CRE maturities totaling $1.5 trillion through year-end pose refinancing risks for regional lenders, but stabilizing property prices and strong capital ratios post-Fed February stress tests temper concerns. Key catalysts include Q1 earnings releases through May and the June FOMC meeting on rate path.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-16 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-16 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Upsets would require a major scandal, health issue for Latimer, or extraordinary national Republican wave before the November 3 general election, though such barriers remain steep.

The Charlize Theron survival thriller debuted massively, holding firm behind newcomer Swapped—an animated family hit voiced by Michael B. Jordan that surged to #1 on daily charts via fresh release buzz and broad appeal. Realistic upsets would require a dramatic late-week surge from those challengers before Sunday's chart lock, though historical hold patterns for action thrillers like Apex suggest stability.

markets.question: Will Arsenal finish in 2nd place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's five-point lead atop the Premier League table—79 points from 36 matches with a +42 goal difference—positions them as heavy title favorites, driving trader consensus to price Manchester City at 86% implied probability for second place. City sit on 74 points from 35 games (+40 GD), nine points clear of third-placed Manchester United, securing their podium spot regardless of results. Recent developments, including Arsenal's victory over West Ham that prompted pundits to call the title race over, have widened the gap after City's earlier 2-1 April win over the Gunners. City's game in hand against Crystal Palace offers a chance to close, but Arsenal's superior run-in and form make an overtake slim; City dropping points while Arsenal wins both finals would lock in the pecking order.

markets.question: Gemini 3.2 released by May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Google's Gemini 3.2 model is made available to the general public by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Gemini 3.2 refers to a product explicitly named Gemini 3.2, or a variant that is recognized as a direct successor to Gemini 3.1, similar to the progression from Gemini 3 to Gemini 3.1. (e.g., Gemini 3.2 GA, Gemini 3.2/3.3/3.4, etc., in any variant, like Pro/Deep Think/Flash/Flash-lite, would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market).

Specialized models for non-text modalities such as video generation (e.g., Veo), image generation (e.g., Imagen, Nano Banana), music generation (e.g., Lyria), or robotics (e.g., Gemini Robotics) will NOT qualify, even if released under the Gemini 3.2 version number. Products labeled as a GA promotion of an already-existing Preview model (e.g., Gemini 3.1 GA) or a new flagship generation (e.g., Gemini 4) or similar that are not explicitly labeled as described above will NOT qualify.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by Google as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company's official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Google, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Google DeepMind has not officially released Gemini 3.2 as of mid-May 2026, with the latest stable models being Gemini 3 Pro and 3.1 Pro Preview from February, fueling trader anticipation for an imminent announcement at Google I/O on May 19-20. Recent leaks, including screenshots of Gemini 3.2 Flash and Omni variants in the Gemini iOS app, highlight promised advances in coding, 3D simulations, animation, and reasoning benchmarks—positioning it to challenge OpenAI's GPT-5 and Anthropic's Claude 4 in the multimodal AI race. Competitive pressures from xAI's Grok and Meta's Llama updates underscore Google's need to demonstrate frontier capabilities, while API changelogs and developer previews signal scaling readiness; watch I/O keynotes for release confirmation or timeline shifts that could swing market-implied odds.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump dance on May 13, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump dances between 12:00 AM ET and 11:59 PM ET on the specified date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Dancing" is defined as deliberate, rhythmic body movement typically matched music or a beat, such as swaying, stepping, or coordinated hand or body motions. Casual gesturing, clapping, or incidental body movement will not qualify. Dancing without the presence of music or a beat will qualify.

AI-generated content, deepfakes, or altered footage will not be considered. 

Videos posted on his social media, which were filmed outside this market's time frame, will not qualify.

This market will resolve based on video footage.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's signature fist-pump "Trump dance," synced to YMCA and a viral pop culture meme from rally celebrations, drives daily Polymarket trader consensus on whether he'll perform deliberate rhythmic moves publicly on specific dates. Recent momentum stems from his confirmed May 5 White House South Lawn appearance during the Presidential Fitness Challenge, where video footage captured him teaching high school athletes the steps amid millions of social media views, plus a full routine at the May 1 The Villages rally. Absent verified dances in the past week amid a quieter schedule—including potential travel—probabilities stay low for non-event days but surge for announced speeches. Watch White House itineraries and rally alerts for catalysts, with resolution hinging on clear, unaltered video evidence by 11:59 PM ET.

markets.question: Getafe CF vs. RCD Mallorca: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Getafe CF and RCD Mallorca, scheduled for May 13 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Getafe CF and RCD Mallorca combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Getafe CF, sitting 6th in La Liga with 44 points after 33 matches, hosts relegation-threatened RCD Mallorca (17th, 35 points) on May 13 in a pivotal late-season encounter carrying Europa Conference League aspirations for the hosts and survival urgency for the visitors. Key absences shape trader consensus, including Getafe's top scorer Borja Mayoral (knee injury, long-term) and Juanmi (injury), alongside Mallorca's striker Vedat Muriqi (injury) and defender Antonio Raíllo (ankle surgery until June). Getafe boasts a narrow recent head-to-head edge (1-0 win last meeting), but Mallorca's dismal away form—one win in 16—contrasts Getafe's inconsistent home results, heightening focus on low-scoring trends and defensive matchups amid both sides' injury-hit attacks.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Ilia Topuria at 75.5% implied probability to remain UFC Lightweight champion through year-end, anchored by his dominant knockout of former titleholder Charles Oliveira to claim the vacant belt at UFC 317 last summer, marking his seamless transition from two-division mastery after featherweight triumphs over Max Holloway and Alexander Volkanovski. Topuria's undefeated record, elite striking, and grappling depth position him as the pound-for-pound elite with no immediate threats, despite a brief personal hiatus into early 2026; he plans a title defense this spring or summer. Arman Tsarukyan (13%) gains traction via wrestling pedigree and recent submission win over Dan Hooker, while Justin Gaethje's (12.6%) interim title capture against Paddy Pimblett in January underscores his knockout power and ranking atop the division. Charles Oliveira (11.8%) lingers as a veteran BJJ threat post-loss, but the market reflects Topuria's unchallenged reign amid quiet contender positioning in recent rankings updates.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-25 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-25 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democratic Rep. Joseph Morelle's commanding trader consensus at 93.5% stems from NY-25's D+10 partisan lean, his consistent general election victories—including 61% in 2024—and dominant fundraising edge ahead of the June 23 closed primaries, where challengers Cassandra Lin Leach, Sherita Traywick, and Robin Wilt trail far behind. Forecasters like Cook Political Report rate the Rochester-area seat Solid Democratic, reflecting no competitive polling and Republican nominee Virginia McIntyre's limited resources as a Monroe County legislator. Recent candidate filings post-April 6 deadline and Morelle's House Administration Committee role solidify this positioning. A Republican upset would require a primary surprise, major Democratic scandal, or overwhelming national midterm wave.

Despite Henan sitting 13th in the Chinese Super League table with eight points from eight games (two wins, two draws, four losses; goal difference -4), their recent momentum contrasts Shenzhen's higher sixth-place standing (nine points; three wins, five losses; goal difference -4), marked by a heavy 5-1 opening loss to Chengdu Rongcheng and defensive vulnerabilities (15 goals conceded). No major injuries reported, positioning this as a closely contested matchup with draw potential amid both teams' inconsistent early-season form.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Despite Prime Minister Keir Starmer's late April announcement pledging legislation to enable proscription of state-backed groups like Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) under the Terrorism Act 2000, no bill has advanced through Parliament by mid-May, with the official proscribed list unchanged since January and excluding the IRGC. The UK's legal framework currently bars designating state entities as terrorist organisations, requiring new laws, Home Secretary order, and affirmative parliamentary resolution—processes traders view as too protracted for completion by June 30 amid competing legislative priorities. Cross-party calls and EU's February designation add pressure, but lack of progress since the pledge drives 89.8% "No" consensus, with rapid bill introduction as the main shift catalyst.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Helsinki be 9°C or below on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fi/vantaa/EFHK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Finnish Meteorological Institute forecasts project Helsinki's May 13 high near 11–13°C under persistent cloud cover and light rain chances from a passing low-pressure trough, aligning trader consensus with tightly clustered 40–41% implied probabilities across 13–17°C outcomes amid short-range uncertainty typical of spring frontal systems. Ensemble spreads from ECMWF and GFS models diverge by 3–5°C due to variable timing of shower bands, southerly wind gusts up to 7 m/s, and potential partial clearing, which could elevate peaks toward 15–16°C or suppress to 11–12°C if overcast dominates. Climatological May highs average 14°C, but current cooler North Atlantic pattern moderates expectations; watch FMI's May 12 update for refined guidance.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Cristiano Ronaldo announce his retirement in 2026?

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the highest IPO Market Cap 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the company that achieves the highest market capitalization in U.S. dollars based on the official closing price on its first trading day in 2026.

This market will resolve to a company that completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If two or more companies have exactly equal highest closing market capitalizations, this market will resolve to the company whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In case the respective company's primary exchange’s official listing page does not report in U.S. dollars, it will be converted to U.S. dollars using the U.S. Federal Reserve Board’s H.10 foreign exchange reference rate for the relevant currency pair on the company’s first trading day (https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h10/). If no such rate is available for the relevant trading day, the most recent previously published rate will be used. If the relevant currency is not listed, another credible exchange rate source will be used.

A listed company may resolve to "No" as soon as it becomes unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the respective companies’ first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Hurupay FDV above $30M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Hurupay's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Hurupay (https://x.com/HurupayApp) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".
